description.p: Sizes were wrong in v1.14.
Avoiding UI overflow in OnePlus 6 with Phosh, my new phone. Just hoping the outage in Spain allows me to push.

Changes in sizes to fix that our new responsive standards were broken because the main menu was not compiling with them.
Fixing feedback labels to small in Hiragana and Katakana, Kanji remains broken in that sense...
Fixes for Windows and adding XMPP as a possible communication mean.
Responsive fixes and clarifying the Kanji payment status.
Adding brand new website and porting to windows. Salud y República. Happy 4/14.
Adding setting fail penalty.
Adding the worst ever settings menu.
Updating to GNOME 48, perl 5.40.1 and updating every cpan library.
Fixing unable to see Japanese characters while having installed some system fonts. This error is known to happen in Tuxedo OS in version v1.05 while noto-cjk is uninstalled. (Thanks Vaun).
Avoiding window resize on hiragana and katakana exercises.
Fixing bug unable to select specific level and decreasing number of simultaneus kanji to learn.
Improving further the description to make know users about they are supposed to fail when they are starting because I got a bad review complaining about that.
Indicating there is kanji support in the description.
Bugfix, avoiding blocking the program for a small time while waiting for the number of kanji characters to populate.
Adding BETA Kanji support.
Adding discord community to retrieve feedback from users.
Migrating to XS because Inline::C is giving compatibility issues.
Bundling noto-cjk by default to not having to ask users to install third party fonts.
Avoiding skip without reading the correct answer with a timeout.
Improving select buttons in dyslexia mode and general contrast.
Fixing regression nothing works because of uncommitted file.
Improving mode for dyslexia.
Adding mode for dyslexia.
Bugfix refactor unable to exit lesson.
Major refactor and new windows before entering a lesson and after leaving it.
There is now an score that meassures how much effort you gave to the app.
The last small display fixes went badly this version tries to smooth that fixes.
On high score characters now the program can ask you to type the character in romanji, that's because it is very important that you can identify characters without clues.
Increasing adaptativity in small display devices.
Adding updated screenshots.
Exit lesson popup is now an adwaita alert, custom styles for light and dark themes.
Adding branding colours
Improving screenshots, adding the option to exit the lesson without exiting the program
Adding a donation button, please use it
Providing dark theme and using libadwaita to have better integration with GNOME
Initial version
This application helps you to learn Japanese characters using a gradual learning algorithm which makes you test-like questions.
You are expected to fail while starting, look up the correct answer when you fail, it helps you to learn faster. You can use this app even if you do not know anything.
It keeps a inner score of how much you know a character to give priority to ask you again about it if needed and introduces new characters when you are ready.
BETA Kanji support available.
It works now without having to install any third party font.

There is a settings menu that allows you to tune your learning further to your rithm, the numbers are saved with intro, if you leave a field empty you will restore the default, good if you mess up with the settings.

screenshot.caption: Success screen after resolving a katakana exercise
Success on a guess romanji exercise
Asking for kanji Reading in On
